headlines.text: Trump and Vance virtually sign US-Iran memorandum of understanding; Strait of Hormuz to partially reopen
Netanyahu says Israel won't abide by US-Iran ceasefire deal, vows to stay in Lebanon security zones
SpaceX acquires AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion just four days after blockbuster Nasdaq IPO
Oil prices plunge to 3-month lows as Iran peace deal raises hopes of restored supply through Hormuz
G7 leaders open summit in France with Ukraine, Middle East, and Iran deal dominating the agenda
World Cup Day 6: France vs Senegal, Argentina vs Algeria headline four matches across the US today
German World Cup fan 'Freddy' goes viral for documenting American culture one Waffle House visit at a time
Senate debates Iran deal terms; Schumer, Thune spar over transparency as full MOU text still unreleased
AAA: National gas average holds near $4.07 per gallon as oil prices fall on Iran peace optimism
Scary Movie 6 craters 71% in its second weekend despite franchise-record $55M opening two weeks ago

breaking.text: BREAKING: Trump signs US-Iran memorandum of understanding; Strait of Hormuz to partially reopen as 60-day nuclear talks begin

stories.hed: US and Iran Sign Peace MOU; Netanyahu Goes Rogue Anyway
stories.dek: Trump and Vance virtually signed a memorandum of understanding with Iran to end the US blockade, reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and kick off 60 days of nuclear negotiations. Netanyahu, not invited to the negotiations and apparently fine with it, immediately pledged Israel would not be bound by the deal and that its 'struggle is not over.'

stories.hed: G7 Summit Opens in France as World Cup, Iran Deal, and Ukraine All Compete for Airtime
stories.dek: G7 leaders convened in Evian-les-Bains, France, with Ukrainian President Zelenskyy joining the talks and Egyptian President el-Sissi urging Israel to abandon plans to seize 70% of Gaza. With a peace deal in hand and a World Cup underway, world leaders are trying to hold many plates at once.

stories.hed: SpaceX Buys AI Coding Startup Cursor for $60B Just Days After IPO
stories.dek: Four trading days after its record-shattering Nasdaq debut, SpaceX signed a definitive agreement to acquire Anysphere, the maker of AI coding tool Cursor, for $60 billion in stock. The deal is SpaceX's first major M&A move as a public company and signals Elon Musk's intent to catch up with Anthropic and OpenAI in the AI tools market.

stories.hed: Senate Demands to See the Iran Deal, Which Nobody Has Seen
stories.dek: With Vance promising the full text of the US-Iran MOU will be released 'this week,' senators from both parties are pressing the White House for details. Minority Leader Schumer took to the floor to address Iran, the Anti-Weaponization Fund, childcare, and elections — a sentence that summarizes 2026 perfectly.

stories.hed: Iran 'Won' the War It Didn't Start, Per CSIS; Netanyahu Got the Bill
stories.dek: Analysts at CSIS say both the US and Iran will claim victory but both have lost in important ways, with the framework deal kicking harder issues down the road. Former Israeli PM Ehud Barak put it more bluntly, saying Iran emerged stronger and Israel emerged weaker — and that is Netanyahu's 'strategic responsibility.'

markets.context: Monday's Wall Street surge on US-Iran peace news carried into Tuesday with tech stocks pulling back slightly while oil cratered to a 3-month low on expectations the Strait of Hormuz will reopen.

gas.note: Gas is down from its May war-panic peak of $4.55, which is a relief, though it's still $1.26 more per gallon than it was before the war started — so your road trip math is still wrong.

popCulture.hed: Scary Movie 6 Implodes at Box Office With 71% Second-Weekend Drop Despite Franchise-Record Opening
popCulture.dek: The Wayans Brothers parody sequel opened to a franchise-record $55 million two weekends ago, then fell off a cliff with a staggering 71.3% drop in week two — one of the worst sophomore plunges in Hollywood history. Audiences apparently showed up on day one for nostalgia and told no one else to bother.

genZ.definition: A German World Cup fan named Freddy has become the tournament's breakout star by documenting every stop of his American road trip on social media — rating Waffle House as 'the holy land,' losing his mind at Buc-ee's, and attracting hundreds of thousands of followers who are watching him discover the US one chain restaurant at a time. He is the content nobody knew they needed.
genZ.usage: Finding out the Iran peace deal text is still secret and your senator hasn't read it either: pure Freddy-discovering-Buc-ee's energy.

tldr: The US and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding to end the war, reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and start nuclear talks, sending oil prices cratering and stocks soaring — though Netanyahu immediately announced Israel wasn't invited to and would not be bound by the deal. In other news, SpaceX bought a $60 billion AI startup four days after going public, because apparently that's just how Tuesdays work now.
